title: leptospermum polygalifolium subsp. howense
intro: Leptospermum polygalifolium subsp. howense, also known as the Howes' Leptospermum, is a subspecies of the Leptospermum polygalifolium plant, which is a species of flowering plant in the family Myrtaceae. This plant is native to New South Wales, Australia, and is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds. The health benefits of Leptospermum polygalifolium subsp. howense include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and provide relief from colds and flu due to its antibacterial and antiviral properties. Herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which can be used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
